channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-05-29 16:28 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284628
>>62284605
agreed. But i still find that those snes games and other old gen platforms were extremely expensive. Obviously at the time it was the best video games available so people were happy to pay but that's still very expensive, especially if you play some sega master system who aged so poorly you can't even be bother to play them 2 hours. 130++$ for a game like that was super expensive.

62284656
>>62284628
I just explained it you dumb faggot. READ

62284666
>>62284656
you didn't explain shit, it was very expensive, a bunch of plastic and one mass produced circuit board isn't worth that much

62284681
>>62284666
0 iq mongrel. Kys

62284717
>>62284681
same fagging phone poster, opinion discarded

62284778
>>62283119
I think you'd just rent most of your games from a video store and then get like one or two for Christmas. Also people would share games with their friends because they weren't socially-handicapped tiktok scrollers.

62285855
>>62284778
rent games? so lame. did anyone actually do that? you buy and resell second hand, game cost you 0 basically that's what i used to do with my ps2

62286204
>>62284628
>at the time it was the best video games available so people
Home video games sucked compared to arcade games and everybody knew it, unless you had a Neo Geo (but each game was like $700 in today’s money)

62286216
>>62286204
Street Fighter II for SNES was amazing despite not being arcade where some nignog could chimp out after getting assblasted by Blanka

62286230
>>62285855
>rent games? so lame.
Less so than selling your own games.

>did anyone actually do that?
Yes and it was amazing, especially when renting those esoteric JP games and playing them with an adapter. It was like temporarily unlocking a new world, you were inviting all your friends to explore these games to the fullest for the weekend.

Of course a lonely consoomer from 2026 can’t get it.

62286236
>>62286216
It was very enjoyable indeed, but still not as good as the arcade version.

62286393
>>62283119
My most sacred memory is when my mom picked me up from 1st grade in 1987 and as we are driving home she told me that she got me a new video game where you can SAVE the game and start from there when you come back to it. WOW.

62286541
>>62283119
When I got my first PC in 1997 at 14 I rarely bought games after that. I had consoles but I didn't have money to buy games and my parents wouldn't just buy me any game I wanted whenever.

62286565
>>62283119
They're bitching just to bitch. There's also like 1000 ways to emulate those games and play them like the originals or better, so really they're retardedly nostalgic to have THE cartridge and then mad a bunch of other people are too

62286669
>>62283133
Nobody is talking about used video games. They're crying because modern consoles are approaching a thousand bucks and moden games are heading towards 100. But anon correctly noted that back in the day, it was $50-60 for a new NES cartridge or a new computer game on three 360kb diskettes, and at the time minimum wage was something like $4 an hour. Adjusted for inflation, a game like Super Mario that you could beat in an hour, or Kings Quest that lasts you a couple of hours max, cost $161.

62287647
>>62283131
>There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.
This
I bought a movie rental stores stock for peanuts when they were going broke in 2012, walked in and offered 2000$ cash for the lot (about 5000 DVDs and blu rays and 2500 PS1, PS2 and 360 games). It was a niche movie buff DVD and game store. Have a pretty complete movie and game library and enjoy it a lot to this day. I am randy marsh.

62287668
>>62286669
>Nobody is talking about used video games.
OP was literally talking about "today's collector price for most of those games" but okay

62287673
>>62287668
both can come into discussion, but the idea was that game used to be even more expensive than they are today yet people bitch about their free world 10k hours lifetime game selling for half what a super nintendo game used to sell

62287727
>>62283119
I don't know what a SNES is (Nintendo consoles are for faggots), but I used to buy pirated PS1/PS2 games for $5 from some shady dude back in the day. Even back then, only retards would pay $50 for a video game. I see times haven't changed...

62288182
>>62287668
Yes.
>today's colllector price is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new
He's still talking about how prices today are still lower when accounting for inflation, even for used collector games, compared to the price for games back in the day.
And he's right. A nintendo entertainment system with one game (Mario/Duck Hunt) adjusted for inflation cost $450.
A New 386 16 with 1mb of ram and a 20mb hard drive with 16 color graphics and midi sound card cost $8500, adjusted for inflation that's $25,000.
People bitching about prices for games these days really have no idea. Their idea of what things cost back in the day is skewed by a combination television where the latest technology was placed "in the common home" as a deliberate advertisement and a long run of (relatively) dramatically discounted prices from the late 90s until the late 10s.
Even today their expectations are ridiculous: "It shouldn't cost as much as when you had physical media because you can just download it!", like servers and connections that can provide fast 85gb video game downloads to millions of people around the world are not expensive themselves.

62288229
>>62283119
People's real wages were also higher.

62288318
>>62283119
I played that game on that console when I was a kid.

62288477
>>62287673
>>62288182
This is true, video games are cheaper in real terms and even in nominal terms today. Even the "expensive" Switch games are not pricier than Street Fighter II in 1993. But the essential things of life are much more expensive (rent, food), which makes video games comparatively just as hard (or harder?) to afford. Hence the complaining.

The crybabies will also complain when a game is free though:
>waaaah I don’t get enough pulls to get my waifu in Genshin Impact, even though I spent $0 in five years of playing daily

62288623
>>62288477
>>62288318
>>62288182
i had a friend at school who had a dedicated playroom room with every fucking super nes game as well as mega drive. Spoiled brat, parents probably were LOADED. That made me diamond at the time

62288647
>>62283119
vidya felt way more expensive and special. buying a new vidya was an important life decision for many, you had to study that shit, read game magazine articles about vidya to weigh which to invest in, you had to save up money, potentially sell other vidya to frens for a bit of cash (like actual paper bills or even actual metal coins). back then when a neighbor kid got a new vidya it was an event and kids would gather to check it out or ask to borrow it someday. i invested all my money in vidya and bought a new one per month. don't get me started on buying a new console, that felt similar to buying a new fridge, something you needed to buy for basic survival because the old one was just not doing it anymore.

62288698
>>62288623
>>62288647
Kek, back then you knew exactly how many games you had, and could recite the list of all your games like it’s a poem.

Each game was considered a rather big purchase and the games felt more "precious" in general. Also true with the PS1-era games.

62288801
>>62283119
muh inflation is a cope shit tier non-argument.
snes and mega drive games were also printed on very expensive cartridges and game makers also had to pay a lot to retail.
now most sales are digital, so there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved and the market is way bigger than it ever was.

basically kys

62289331
>>62288229
>People's real wages were also higher.
No, they really weren't. The people making this claim base it on the price of Gold in 1970 and extrapolate it from there. (or they compare themselves to characters on 70s and 80s television) The price of Gold was artificially suppressed for 30 years by Bretton Woods. The median salary in 1987 was $30,000 a year. If you made above 60,000 you were upper middle class. Pic related was a middle class home and cost 120k new. People also like to pretend that there weren't oil shocks and 20% inflation in the 70s and 80s wiping out that majority of boomers' savings, or that a mortgage for such a home didn't come with a 16% interest rate.

62289351
>>62288801
>there is literally zero actual scarcity or logistics involved
Servers and high speed connections are not zero logistics.
You don't even put two and two together. In the heady days of the wild west internet of the early 2000s, bandwidth was cheap, but it was also slow. People don't realize that because they demanded HD (and later 4K) streaming that infrastructure costs have skyrocketed so much that only the richest corporations can afford to provide that kind of service. You wonder why there's so much consolidation of media, why the major corporations have bought up so many smaller studios, why social media and video streaming is reduced to a handful of winners, it's because the bandwidth necessary to transmit the large amounts of data you're demanding is huge, and Bandwidth ain't actually cheap at all.

62289407
>>62283119
games used to actually be good, and they provided you with hundreds of hours of entertainment. not the dog shit coming out today that you beat in 8 hrs and then never play again

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 28 replies — 112.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-05-29 16:28 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285966
>>62285713
TP? Where are you? And do you still own BAT?

62286043
>>62285619
bat og here. im heavily underwater with my bat investment at this point but still holding. i'm never going to sell - not because bat will be some kind of currency in the future and i won't need to sell it like some link cope, but I don't want to make profit from BAT anymore and I also don't want anyone to use my tokens for ill purposes. truth be told, after reading that brendan eich in 2006 didn't think that people with xy chromosomes that undergo hrt and srs maybe shouldn't be allowed to compete in female chess tournaments has really had me rethinking my investment and the morality of it. i only want to invest in ethical companies like lockheed martin that actually help make democracy more accessible to global citizens around the world. if over 20 years ago the ceo of brave browser had such a controversial view it makes me concerned that he might have some problematic ideas today. did he even get the covid vaccine? im not sure, and I don't want to find out the hard way. i will simply hold onto my hundreds of thousands of bat, and not sell them, not lend them, just freeze them up on my hard drive in a password i refuse to commit to memory and dispose of my thinkpad into landfill. i encourage all other bat holders with a conscience to do the same.

62286053
>>62286043
Wow. That's some deep pasta bro. Missed this one

62286063
>>62285713
>IT'S BEEN A WHILE
It's been a hot minute. Sup, lad?

>>62285966
>Where are you?
o/
>do you still own BAT?
Of course, lad *sobs *

Haven't seen a thread in a minute. Was planning on doing some thot bait threads when we get a new JanJan.

62286087
>>62286063
>Blue hair
Gross

62286122
>>62286087
I mean if you want me to post men or trannies for you, just give me the word ^_^
_

Anyone else getting some pretty gnarly memory leaks from Brave recently?

62286148
>>62286122
This is better. Thanks. Looks like a boobier version of that Claire Manson fashion bitch on instagram. Thanks TP

62286153
>>62286063
>>62286122
Hawt
Got any uber thicc goths??

62286226
BAT is total shite. You have to be retarded to still believe in this garbage

62286297
>>62285619
if i had invested in LINK instead of BAT i'd be a millionaire right now, and probably retired

62286350
>>62286122
no fucking trannies please.

just hot ass bitches, keep em coming TP, and thanks legend

62286359
>>62286297
If I ate one pile of dogshit instead of another pile of dogshit I'd be puking my brains out harder right now too

62286450
>>62286148
NP. Feel free to drop a request.

>>62286153
Of course!

>>62286297
I absolutely abhor LINK, but same u_u. I literally should've put more money anywhere else.

>>62286350
I'm just fucking around.
>Legend
Much appreciated!

>>62286359
At least STINK pumped. All I wanted was $4 BAT.

62286624
>>62286450
fuark that bitch can get it

62286763
>>62286450
Would happily suffocate devouring her ass while she sits on my face 10/10 slut

62287337
https://www.instagram.com/veronicanakama/

62287362
>>62286450
moar

>>62286297
same, I don't like to think about it.

62289019
>2026
>BAT
wew

62289108
>>62286063
>>62286122
jolly good jiggling jugs TP
>>62286450
seconding moar of this btggf or even her username

62289174
How is this board still discussing dead shitcoins from 10 years ago
Does no one here like making money

62289182
>>62287337
lmao she do be living high off the BAT scam

62289202
>>62289174
BAT threads are just places for us to lament and to talk to the friends we met along the way

62289359
>>62286122
what the fuck is this whore's name

62289403
>>62286122
>gnarly memory leaks
no its working damn fine on my machine
I had to fix youtube by element blocking the share links though

62289404
>>62286122
Man

item.title: This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle. — 41 replies — 103.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-29 16:12 UTC

OP

This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62285843
>eth/sol/bnb
I hope niggy 10xes from here. Not because it would make me rich, but because of all the other shit around it that would pump.

62285851
>>62285810
A non-LINK project? On the LINK board?
They use their own oracles as well?
AND it's only gone up since?

No no no this will not do. Jannies send this nigger to the shadow realm

62285856
>>62285810
buy a fucking ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286218
>>62286151
cheeto mussolini is mostly interested in making money for himself and his friends. In crypto this means shilling some shit that pumps aand then rugs. In stocks it means number only go up.

62286304
>>62286206
OK fair enough. 100 sui 1000 make it we thinking?

62286343
>>62286304
I think 10,000 would be a decent make it stack. There's still a fat chunk of supply that is held under rewards, whether its a 2nd airdrop or staking rewards, no clue

62286514
>>62285810
hype, pump and tao are the big 3. this is a hype, pump and tao board now.

62286671
The nano second the Glamsterdam update releases there will be 3 hyperliquid competitors launching, with instant access to $40 Billion in liquidity on L1 (Hype only have $2 billion). JEFF WE RUE THE DAY HE BETRAYED VITALIK BY LAUNCHING A SPERATE EVM L1.

62286723
This just happened
Jeffrey Sprecher, CEO of ICE (Owners of the NYSE)

62286733
>>62286723
Yeah, it's happening

62286743
>>62286723
>>62286733
Bullish

62286801
>>62285810
60B fdv....

62286820
>>62286801
so what, it isnt random vc niggers getting the unlocks, it's the team and they've shown they dont turbo dump like sirgay and his band of faggots

62286960
>>62286801
Also constant burns

62286967
>>62286820
This is what makes hyperliquid different. It was just created by a turbo autist that loves perp futures trading, he gave everything to the community, no VCs that dump everytime it pumps, no HR team, no marketing, nothing, just a product that works and people actually use it.

It's the most used blockchain, it generates the highest amount of fees and the buyback mechanism makes it pretty hard to crash. Now, with stocks and commodities, everytime there is a happening, you have everyone trading the current thing, gold, silver, oil, IPO's, whatever, and it brings even more people to hyperliquid. It's ATHs so everybody made money with hype.

One of the safest bets right now in crypto.

62286984
I waiting for the big drop in November so I will fill my pockets with SOLANA and hold it until 2029.

62286986
>>62286967
How much are they paying you, shill? Fuck off.

62287228
>>62286986
Your chart, baggie

62287229
>>62287228
ouch

62287266
>>62285810
>the next cycle
whos gonna tell him?

62287572
>>62285810
Bad optics. Its the new I SEE PEE
Make your money and run

62288269
>>62287572
Hyperliquid is an actual product that many people use. ICP is just a white paper, it's not even close to being the same thing.

62288315
>>62287228
im selling it all at the next little green candle we are gettin,

62288348
one rule: if /biz/ fades a coin, get in. They're generational bagholders for a reason, fucking losers

62288368
>>62288348
take your one rule and shove it up your ass retard

62288403
>>62288368
found the link bagholder, kys nigger

62289122
>>62286206
Another ATH
In hindsight King Trump did make it obvious. This just in this morning

62289153
So close

62289167
How high?

62289181
New ALL TIME HIGH. People that invested in hype don't know what a bear market is.

SIMPLE AS.

62289232
>>62289167
Guaranteed: $700
Optimistic: $1500
Schizo: $5000+

62289310
Top 10 coin about to flip Top 9 on ATH and there's still just 4-5 posters on this board actually invested and regularly posts about it on biz

Yea I'm thinking we're flipping the evil chink's coin...

item.title: how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking? — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
how do you avoid your infos being leaked ans the subsequent home jacking?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-29 16:35 UTC

OP

France has seen an explosion in crypto kidnapping. This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances. There also was a data leak from some kind of private investment company after a hack.
People with balances as low as 2 btc have been targetted and successfully forced to give up all their assets under threats. Some people living abroad have had their relatives kidnapped.

How do you avoid this shit? all the regulations on crypto have been an absolute disaster, the only end goal is to steal your wealth via taxation and state control. This totally ruins the whole crypto thing. There is no point in holding large amounts of cryptos if it puts a target on your back and on your familly and you can't sleep well at night.
Now many anons might think this is a french thing but eventually more leaks will happen and nobody will be safe. there will be lists with your name and address and all the purchase you did in the last years, even maybe your depo address that criminals will be able to track and see what most likely happened with your balance.

One simple mitigation measure would be to use many exchanges, many adresses, as to make it appear you hold less, but this is also an annoying process that require strict management and doesn't guarantee additional leaks at the state level if they group all your trading infos.
You should stop using any KYC, stop paying taxes on government that don't care at all about your safety, and go full down the crypto ecosystem giving up off ramps as much as you can.

Early replies (first 3)

62287742
Generic India hate thread disguised as something else.

62287753
>>62287731
>France
That's what you get for living in Africa.

62287759
>>62287753
while i can't disagree, the problem remains. if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu. Usa is known for its violent crime just wait til a list of easy 1 Mil++ robberies on individual citizens start going around and see what happens

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287821
>>62287759
>if you think living in the usa or germany makes you safe from this issue you're delulu.
well, those places are also shitholes, but they don't "demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances"
kill retarded communists

62287831
>>62287753
agreed, the french deserve 100% of what they get, they STILL advocate for communism and whine about the 'rich' not paying their share, even though the vast majority of their rich fled that shithole years ago.

62287835
>>62287731
Dont live in 3rd world countries like france and obviously most kidnappings happen due to money flex not leaks

62287837
the leftists do this. The other half doesn't but get jewed constantly.
anyway that's a stupid argument

62287850
>>62287753
France voted for it, they deserve all they get and more.

62287859
>>62287850
>>62287821
>>62287831

While your answers are completely selfish and retarded, i took a visit to rubic and rubic actually allows cross chain swaps between bitcoin and many others stables so that's one way you can do crypto to crypto trade without KYC, and sell high to rebuy lower without every retard gov agency being aware of it.
I'm there are a lot of solutions out there.

62287875
>>62287731
the gov home address leaks are the insane part of this coz you cant really prevent that without not reporting (which is kinda impossible if you ever want to spend a decent amount)

only way to prevent i guess is having a holiday home and just pointing to that

62287892
>>62287821
Database leaks happen all the time. Its just a matter of time before a KYC exchange gets breached and your info gets leaked

62287918
>>62287731
>vidrel
Poor wagie, it sucks that everything is filmed now. He can't forget this even if I bet he wants to.

62287930
>>62287731
>This explosion came after the state demanded citizens to declare their "self hosted" wallets and their balances
All by design

62287931
>>62287731
>I have a lot of money in crypto/cash/gold
>Omg why am I a target for robbery?!
What did you expect, faggot? Life like this. It's the way it is

62287985
>>62287731
1. lie
2. do not ever disclose your balance
3. have 2 homes in case

62288016
>>62287931
very forward thinking anon.
Difference is with a bank account it's much more anonymous and you can't just get robbed in a wrench key attack.

62288126
>>62287892
>KYC exchange
retard. THE FUCKING STATE IS MAKING YOU DO IT

62288135
>>62287985
>GOY ID
Kek has spoken.

62288136
>>62287759
>easy robberies
Nigga I will kill anyone who comes around trying to rob me. I am armed and insane.

62288150
>>62288136
Yeah, imagine giving up your seed phrase to some angry African because it has a gun that it barely knows how to use.

62288560
>>62287731
that video brought me so much joy, thank you.

62289395
>>62288560
i couldnt imagine a stupider gimmick than a rollerblade setup in a high-end, indoor restaurant, where alot of soup, stew and curry-style foodstuffs are served.

It makes sense in an outdoors setting like in america, where you're serving lightweight packaged food, and its probably more efficient overall to use rollerblades. But this is pajeet-tarded.

62289432
>>62289395
Rollerblade service should only be done by red headed tomboys with short hair and freckles with a small chest, a bubble butt, and wide hips.

item.title: The average IQ on /biz/ has been dropping by like 5 points every year for a long time. — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The average IQ on /biz/ has been dropping by like 5 points every year for a long time.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-29 16:25 UTC

OP

The average IQ on /biz/ has been dropping by like 5 points every year for a long time.

We're at like 85 right now

Early replies (first 3)

62288688
>>62288672
disagree. The board is now peak aryan 130 iq minimum. it's dead, only the purest aryans remain

62288737
Every bullrun a lot of intelligent anons make it and leave forever, and a lot of new get-rich-quick retards arrive to fill their place. The board becoming dumber and dumber is by design

62288747
>>62288688
CHECKED, MAGIC DUBS RIGHT HERE

I’m Jewish though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62288762
>>62288737
I’m here since day 1 of /biz/ and it was always retarded, always full of get-rich-quick opportunists, etc.

The intelligent Anons are still around and very easy to find, because they’re called retards or Jews as soon as they post anything

62288853
>>62288672
This board is about dumping your grandparents savings into shitcoins and hoping one day it's gonna repeat the fate of the bitcoin because a twitter checkmark fag said it's pulling investors or something. The initial base IQ was barely in 2 digits bracket, there's nowhere to fall

62288883
>>62288853
It was already this board’s theme back then in 2015, when BTC dropped to $200 after reaching $1,100 earlier

>you missed the opportunity bro, it’s too late, it will never go 500x again

62288928
>>62288737
>Every bullrun a lot of intelligent anons make it
max IQ to believe this?

62288948
>>62288928
>everyone is as bad as me at trading and investing
Okay dude

62288971
>>62288747
i'm sorry to hear that.
the dubs have spoken however

62288982
>>62288737
So you assume once a person made money on a bullrun they'd just never post here again rather than sticking around on the place they were using when they made a bunch of money?

62288987
>>62288688
Yeah, there are some really smart anons here. They only appear dumb because they shitpost.

62288989
Communism is one of history’s most catastrophic political experiments: an ideology painted as paradise but repeatedly collapsing into fear, poverty, and authoritarian control. It promises equality for all, yet time after time it has produced societies where citizens stand equally miserable while powerful party elites live above the rules they impose on everyone else.
Under communist regimes, individuality becomes suspicious, religion becomes dangerous, free speech becomes rebellion, and the state becomes an all-seeing force demanding loyalty above family, faith, and conscience. From endless propaganda posters to secret police and crushing censorship, communism has often transformed nations into giant machines where human beings are treated less like souls and more like replaceable gears.
Economically, communist systems have repeatedly stumbled into shortages, inefficiency, corruption, and stagnation. History is filled with stories of empty shelves, ration lines, black markets, and citizens risking everything just to escape. The dream of a worker’s utopia too often became a nightmare of bureaucracy, surveillance, and centralized control.
Its critics argue that communism suffers from a fatal flaw: it assumes enormous power can be trusted to governments without corrupting them. In reality, concentrating total economic and political control in one system has repeatedly opened the door to repression on a massive scale.
What begins as a promise of equality can quickly become a system where freedom disappears, dissent is silenced, and the state demands absolute obedience in exchange for broken promises.

62288991
>>62288948
good point. how many millions are you worth?

62289007
I think it's because the pedophile elites trying to blend in left and went into hiding. Bring back cunny posting leet hackers and finance insiders and make 4chin great again

62289008
>>62288982
It’s not wrong though, why would you keep on talking to nazi schizo retards shilling ICP or LINK, when you just made $15 million by pulling the rug under them?

62289018
the easy money narrative and GMEtards refusing to leave caused the board to fall apart. Now it's either people who realized they wouldn't make it and decided to careermax fudding anything that isn't the safest portfolio ever, people who made it on bitcoin 10 years ago and never sold and a whole slew of retards and underperformers ruing their terrible luck.

It makes it extremely difficult to stock pick or crypto pick now.

62289053
>>62289018
But it’s easier than ever to pick stocks and crypto:

>follow the trends
>anticipate what’s going to be in high demand within these trends
>buy low
>sell high
>don’t fall for obvious scams
>don’t buy something that is useless and a net negative to society
>don’t be greedy and don’t be afraid

If after reading this, you’re thinking "yep, I’m going all in on a shitcoin that’s down 98% since 2017" instead of buying AI, quantum and space stocks, I can’t help you

62289067
>>62289053
No I'm reading this and seeing someone who clearly has a sub10k portfolio talking out their ass, which is par for the course with nu-/biz/.

62289069
>>62289008
Just because you're a failure doesn't mean every room you're in is full of failures.

62289100
>>62289067
Wrong and wrong, try again

>>62289069
Is this the moment where I call you a jeet and a moron?

62289109
>>62289100
when*

62289119
>>62289100
Probably, because all you have is screeching and namecalling for some reason.

62289131
>>62289100
And I'd like to point out you claimed people who make it wouldn't be here, so you don't get to turn around and insist the guy calling you poor is wrong. You already claimed you wouldn't be here if you had wealth.

62289382
>>62289119
>calls me a failure
>"hey stop the namecalling yo"

>>62289131
That’s not what I wrote at all. People who made it big through dubious get-rich-quick schemes have no reason to keep on looking for new schemes in this cesspool, so it’s not surprising if a fair number of successful Anons from the previous crypto cycles disappeared. It doesn’t mean everyone here is a failure.

Personally I’m not into crypto scamming and I stay here for anthropological reasons. What about you?

item.title: Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 22 replies — 22.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-29 15:36 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62284484
>>62283370

62284489
Please fuck my ass

62284919
>>62284489
you say that every day, creep.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284922
>>62283370
ICP is finished OP and I say that as a cucked ICP holder. All their technical people are leaving and the price chart will forever look like the euthanasia roller coaster until the price hits 3 cents. They had an opportunity to become THE AI chain and totally fumbled the opportunity with mismanagement. I have no faith in Dominic's strategic direction at this point. I'm dissolving my 6month neuron with the hopes to sell by EOY to write off a loss for tax purposes.

62284936
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62284942
>>62284922
ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan, Switzerland and UN. Also has a partnership with HBAR, Maestro and Elon Musk’s brother is one of the holders.
ICP has one of the fastest growing developers and more thanks to Caffeine AI.
Some shit coins like kaspa only have 5 active devs while Dfinity has 200 + and thousands of people building every day.

The price in 2021 was manipulated, you are a cocksuckers.

62284948
>>62284922
>ICP is finished OP

* you are a cocksucker

62284992
>>62284942
>ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan

62284996
>>62284922
When you sell because of disinformation, just don’t kill yourself when ICP does a 100x.

62285005
>>62284992
80 million people is a joke to you? At least it’s being utilized by someone unlike 99% of snakeoil out there like Solana that don’t do anything besides memes.

62285370
>>62284992
.

62285887
>>62284992
.

62285944
>>62284942
>thousands of people building every day
This is just a plain lie. Nobody wants to build with gay Motoko. Andreas Rossberg left Dfinity 4 years ago now, and CTO Jan left last year. It's over for ICP's development. Single-line commits on github don't count worth shit.
>>62284996
Selling before it goes to $0.03

62285947
>>62284922
none of that matters for the price to go up

62285963
>>62285944
The chart doesn’t lie. Not only are people building on ICP but Caffeine made it possible for thousands of projects to be stored on the internet computer web service.

eat shit

62285965
>>62285944
cope

62285982
>>62285963
>>62285965
TOPKEK
so desperate for any attention

62285988
>>62285963
Caffeine is a grift scam by Lomesh the scammer and Dom, after they realized their multi-million dollar throwaway project HotOrNot (now YRAL) is a failure.

62286401
>>62285988
You realize that you can make video games on caffeine? How in fuck is that a scam? I fucking used it. You people really got nothing better to do than spread disinformation

62286421
>>62286401
Calm down sperg, caffeine is expensive and not a practical solution for anyone. That's why it hasn't made any impact CBR in anyways since it became publicly available. Cope and seethe all you want, but ICP is over. Dominic Williams is not a good person

62287613
>>62286421
Piss off. There is no crypto that can do what ICP does. 99% of altcouns can’t even store a phone photo. You’re just a hater who probably lost money.

62289113
>>62287613
>You’re just a hater who probably lost money.
There isn't a single ICP investor who hasn't lost a shitload of money to pay for this man's all-expense-paid luxury lifestyle in Switzerland, complete with a Porsche 911.

item.title: Chinese tech stocks — 16 replies — 20.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chinese tech stocks

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-29 15:48 UTC

OP

These last couple of years has been pretty generational for American tech stocks. Anything addressing the AI rollout has pretty much gone vertical. Mag7 is at ath, MU, SNDK, etc. all up 10-20x in a year. And there is plenty more. This is on the back of massive private and public AI spending (7.8tril by 2028 or whatever). These were great opportunities and could very well continue to go up.

I've started looking at other countries. KOSPI up almost 200% in a year, NIKKEI almost 70% in a year. However I have noticed Chinas market is pretty much flat/slightly down. I know China is dealing with various issues (stagflation, unemployment, low productivity, gov intervention etc.), but this feels like a massive discrepancy.

I'm gathering a list of some solid Chinese tech stocks as I think they have the most room to grow. In particular I'm liking BABA, BAIDU, CQQQ and XIOAMI. These companies are all exposed to AI growth, cloud, compute and data centre rollout across China and the world. If these companies were in the USA they would automatically be trading close to 10x they are now just cause thats the norm.

I am also looking at Vietnamese tech stocks too but thats more niche. I think the US market continues to do well and thats fine, but the bulk of the money has been made. Chinese tech stocks is my prediction and I would love some input as to why these aren't a good buy.

Early replies (first 3)

62286501
>>62286463
China doesn’t want people investing into their market. Simple as that.
They don’t want funny money shit.
China values stability. You can invest into their blue chips I guess.

62286558
>>62286501
Stability will mean China loses the AI race and enriches their biggest rival. I understand it may have been that way, but at some point they need to compete. The companies are solid, revenue and infrastructure. It almost feels like at some point the blue chips I mentioned will need to pump to compete on the stage with USA.

62286564
>>62286463
If I said the American stock market was a casino, a joke, a farce or a circus, you would know exactly what I mean. (Even if it isn’t 100% true obviously)
The Chinese and their culture don’t want that for their stock maket.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286572
>>62286558
>loses the AI race
there is no race. the AI mania in the west is equivalent to china spending billions on empty uninhabited ghost cities. crony capitalism

62286595
>>62286564
I value your input, I just feel like at some point the difference of 300b for BABA and 3t for AMZN will eventually even itself out, and the Chinese counterparts stand to gain the most in this situation. Just an observation.

>>62286572
AI has revenue to back it up. And China will always need their own versions of tech so they don't overly rely on the west. Sure its crony capitalism but the $$ being transferred between different companies is ridiculous (AI -> hardware -> memory -> AI) and surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.

62286600
>>62286558
>>62286572
Yeah. There isn’t a “race.”
China also has cheap energy apparently.
Also, the fact Bassent and Trump literally go on tv everyday and say
>China is far behind the usa
means China probably isn’t behind or far behind. Even Jensen Huang said “China isn’t behind” and then immediately had to correct what he said.
Since China has cheap emergy, they can just use more chips or whatever. Jensen did a segment on this.

62286604
>>62286595
> surely China has to engage or be left victims to the empire of the USA.
Go watch some Jensen Huang interviews. I am sure he knows more than us. He doesn’t say China is behind.

62286660
>>62286604
I will thank you. But in a way does that not also mean these companies are undervalued? pic rel seems like they are a combination of google and amazon. Or maybe I am just looking at it through the lens of Americanisation.

62286903
>>62286600
>then immediately had to correct what he said
To the gulag with the infidel!

62287114
>>62286660
Idk, the companies are sound for sure but chinese government fuck with companies or do these things. I lost like $6000 on didi. Lmao.
>>62286903
It really isn’t a race but yeah watch his interviews. He explains it better.

62287265
>>62286463

62288363
Bump

62289048
>>62286463
Alibaba is most undervalued. Did you know that they manufacture their own chips? T-Head semiconductors belongs to Alibaba Group.

62289095
>>62286463
Chinese regulators have way less tolerance for market concentration and monopoly/oligopoly profits.

62289180
>>62286660
Corporate valuation is supposed to reflect the present value of all expected future profits. Revenue does not automatically mean profits.

The Chinese government has little tolerance for market concentration and the associated monopoly rents, so, if a private Chinese company manages to get into a position to extract monopoly rents, this is unlikely to be sustained. In a perfectly competitive market, profits tend toward zero.

62289193
>>62286558
Chinese companies do not depend on public capital markets to raise money to the extend US companies do.

"Losing" a "race" assumes there's a finish line and winner-takes-all in an irreversible manner. However, this is not the case, because there is no real moat in AI. The only moat is the artificial moat caused by the export controls on microchip manufacturing equipment, which is a moat the Chinese are going to bridge, so it is not so much a moat as it is speed bump.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-29 15:28 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285029
>>62285022
Solana is a scam.

The only legit alts out there are ICP and their partner HBAR. You can make an argument for XRP Hyperliquid and link

62285041
>>62285022
imagine if this shit somehow end up mooning and i didn't buy any despite your endless shilling while it was 2$

62285042
>6 month old fogo has 3x the transactions of 11 year old eth
true and real

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285065
Betamax has superior picture quality than VHS. Betamax will be the future.

62285158
>>62285041
Yeah it's going to explode, it's going to go to $0.60 and then back up to $2.20 and the board will be full of euphoric ICP bots.

62285239
Utility and token price are not directly connected and when there is a vast amount of premined tokens in reserve just waiting to be dumped its in the companies best interest to suppress the price so that there is higher utilization.

62285307
>INTERNET COMPUTER


Cycles / s
33,667,085,111
+

Network market cap
$1,565,741,556
+
§Internet Computer Protocol (ICP)
Sovereign internet cloud for AI that builds
Tamperproof · Always-on · Yours

The Internet Computer is a frontier cloud created by a mathematically secure network protocol that combines independently operated compute nodes. Hosted apps and services are immune to infrastructure hacks, and guaranteed to run, so AI agents can build and update them hands-free, without the need for human security and systems teams in the loop. Frontier serverless languages introduce "orthogonal persistence," which enables agents to build sophisticated backends faster, using fewer compute tokens, while preventing lossy app updates that might lose data. Digital assets ready.
This is absolutely incoherent Babble

62285845
>>62285022
i possess ICP and I am a homosexual

62285861
I still believe in Moonman I don’t care what anybody says, he was racist and therefore based so I believe in him fully a racist would never EVER act like a nigger and lie

62286056
>>62285022
The real question is how did crypto somehow miss all of the gains from AI hype. Some coins pumped like 20%-30% off the narra and that was it. Can't help but hold on to hopium that more will come for AI related coins, but I know better teebs.

62287044
>>62285022
better table would be value in satoshis transferred.

62287272
>>62285042
In no universe is this real, what the fuck is this thread?

62287289
>>62286056
Crypto is almost entirely retail, while the stock market has a much higher institutional presence. I guess the current AI push is driven by institutions. What retail does on an entirely different market is unrelated.

62287299
>>62285065
>the great Betamax rug pull of 1975

62287534
>>62285022
I've heard anyone using ICP and their Defi's TVL is just 11.5 million Dollars.
But somehow they 288 billion transactions on chain?

62287667
>>62285022
>most botted blockchain

62288638
>>62285022
how to fuck is bitcoin below fogo? What the fuck is fogo? This has to be a joke

62289066
>>62285022
>token
>not needed

item.title: How much time should u be given off if you have a family death assuming u dont have much PTO left? — 8 replies — 4.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much time should u be given off if you have a family death assuming u dont have much PTO left?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-29 14:56 UTC

OP

How much time should u be given off if you have a family death assuming u dont have much PTO left? Especially cosndierimg most family death doesnt happen instantly and it takes a week if not longer for them to die?


I couldn't work for a week and a half when my dog of 18 years died. I was actually proud of myself that it took me only a week and a half to get over it as it was my like 4th family death and ive gotten better at coping. Despite agreeing to not get paid for the time I missed, they fired me when I got back.
My first family death was my first dog, a cotoyr killed her, I was so fucking distraught but I needed money and went back to work in 2 days. It took me months to get semi back to normal cuz I didnt have the space to grieve. I didnt do shit at work the whole time and they never noticed lol.


>inb4 hurr durr its just a dog

I had human family deaths also, they all hurt.. my grandparents weren't there EVERY day morning and night of my life. They had their own life's and jounries while my dogs lives revolved soley around me.


Anyways, I have ptsd from getting fired like that and have accumulated over 200 hours to that im too afrid to use. Bootlickers like my one of my bosses who came in 1 day after his dad died can rejoice..

Early replies (first 3)

62285935
Forgot to mention, he came in one day after his dad died. All for 80k a year.. Most jobs dont pay enough to start q family or buy a house, yet these bootlicker are about to come out of the woodwork and tell me im the worst pieace of shit on earth just cuz I want a week or two of UNPAID leave when I have a family death.

62287651
Why do you need time off? What exactly are you doing?

62287665
>>62287651
Grieving kek

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287685
>>62287665
Like what's the process? You're crying or what? Or just sad? Can't you do that at work?
Pathetic lol

62287688
>>62287685
I mean 'boohoo my poor doggo'? Just get a new one

62287700
>>62285924
2-days off is the law in australia, and it must be immediate family.

62287708
>>62287700
What are you doing on those two days?
I understand if you had to leave work for a funeral, but I don't understand what people are doing the rest of the time

62288899
>>62287665
The wagies deserve it
